description:
Standalone follow-up after the .29 arc closes. Where the set of fns reachable via a given CallClosure / TailCallClosure is statically bounded (small or singleton), specialize the closure target(s) against the actual callsite arg Descrs. Subsumes fz-ul4.27.5.6 part A (originally framed as "closure-callsite narrowing in ir_typer").

## Why deferred from the arc

The .29 arc collapses the closure-reachable skip into a clean any-key fallback (.29.3). That's correct and sufficient for shipping. Tightening the fallback to monomorphic specialization where flow analysis can bound the callee set is a strict optimization on top of correct behavior.

## Approach

Flow analysis on closure values: track which fns each `Prim::MakeClosure` constructs, propagate through bindings, and at each `Term::CallClosure` site collect the reachable target set. When the set is singleton, treat the callsite as if it were a direct Call against that fn (specialize accordingly). When bounded but plural, optionally instantiate multiple specializations and dispatch. When unbounded, stay with the any-key fallback.

## Supersedes

fz-ul4.27.5.6 part A — that ticket is closed when this lands.

## Blocked by

fz-ul4.29.4.
title: .29.5: Closure-callsite flow analysis
